production_gaps.title: Generic fallback rationale used hardcoded 'category' literal
production_gaps.description: apply_category_rule dispatched unmapped categories through generic_rule which called _status_based_rule('category', evidence), producing rationale 'all category checks passed' instead of using the actual category name.
production_gaps.fix: Changed apply_category_rule to call _status_based_rule(category, evidence) directly for unmapped categories. Added test_unknown_category_rationale_uses_actual_name.

production_gaps.title: evaluate_gate audit integration was untested
production_gaps.description: The audit emission code path in evaluate_gate (lines 266-287) including GateDecision and GateRendered event wiring was never exercised by tests. Individual audit events were tested in test_gate_audit.py but the integration in evaluate_gate was not.
production_gaps.fix: Added EvaluateGateAuditTests with test_audit_events_emitted_when_policy_enabled (verifies both events written to audit log) and test_no_audit_when_policy_absent (verifies no file created).

verified_invariants: Fail-closed: empty evidence -> error status; active categories without evidence -> FAIL; error/timeout statuses -> FAIL
LLM confidence gate: PASS downgrades to CONCERNS on confidence < 5; FAIL is never upgraded
Determinism: sorted category iteration, sorted evidence bundle hashing, deterministic filenames
Waiver exact-match: _check_categories requires waiver failing_categories to exactly match gate's failing categories
NA handling: categories_na produces NA verdict with consistent 5-key shape (verdict, rationale, required, actual, evidence_refs)
No new Python deps: all imports from stdlib + existing internal modules
LOC limits: category_rules 233, verdict_engine 289, gate_audit 137 — all under 500
Audit events ride UnknownEvent forward-compat; telemetry_events.py untouched
